function.name: analyze_data
function.description: Analyze data using natural language

description.description: Natural language description of the analysis to perform

data_source.description: Path to data file or source description

function.name: analyze_image_gemini
function.description: Analyze images using Gemini 2.5 Flash Image vision capabilities. State-of-the-art OCR, object detection, scene understanding, visual Q&A, document analysis, and chart/graph interpretation.

image_path.description: Path to the image file to analyze. Supports PNG, JPG, JPEG, GIF, and WebP formats.

analysis_type.description: Type of analysis: 'general' (scene description), 'ocr' (text extraction), 'objects' (object detection), 'documents' (document analysis), 'qa' (visual question answering), or 'detailed' (comprehensive analysis)

question.description: Specific question about the image (for 'qa' analysis type). What do you want to know about this image?

focus_areas.description: Specific areas or objects to focus on in the analysis. Comma-separated list (e.g., 'text, charts, people, vehicles')

function.name: analyze_video_gemini
function.description: Analyze videos using Gemini 2.5 Flash with File Upload API. Supports local video files up to 2GB, YouTube URLs, and comprehensive video understanding including scene analysis, object detection, content summarization, and visual Q&A.

video_source.description: Path to local video file or YouTube URL to analyze

prompt.description: Analysis prompt or question about the video (e.g., 'Summarize this video', 'What objects are visible?', 'Create a quiz based on this content')

wait_for_processing.description: Whether to wait for video processing to complete (default: True)

function.name: agent_draft
function.description: Draft a specialized agent configuration before saving. Creates a preview in the drafts directory.

agent_id.description: Unique identifier for the agent (lowercase, 2-64 chars of a-z, 0-9, _, -)

description.description: Description of what the agent does

systemPromptAddition.description: Additional system prompt instructions for this agent

mode.description: Operation mode: auto, confirm, read_only, or planning

allowedTools.description: Optional list of allowed tool names (empty array for all tools)

deniedTools.description: Optional list of denied tool names

maxTurns.description: Optional maximum number of turns for this agent

function.name: agent_apply
function.description: Apply a drafted agent configuration, writing it to the agents directory

force_overwrite.description: If true, overwrites existing agent with same ID

cleanup_draft.description: If true, removes the draft file after applying

function.name: ask_oracle
function.description: Escalate only when normal reasoning is exhausted or when a user explicitly requests it. Ask Oracle summons DeepSeek V3.2 via OpenRouter for ultra-hard problems, intricate code debugging, or Oracle-level insight, returning a deeply reasoned answer plus optional reasoning traces.

question.description: Direct question or task for the Oracle to analyze.

context.description: Summaries plus any explicit data the Oracle must know; avoid raw logs to control tokens.

temperature.description: Controls creativity (0.0-1.5). Lower stays precise, higher explores more ideas.

max_output_tokens.description: Upper bound for Oracle's answer length (max 4000).

reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning budget hint (auto/minimal/low/medium/high). Higher = more cost + richer traces.

include_reasoning.description: Return DeepSeek's reasoning traces when available.

function.name: command_draft
function.description: Draft a custom command before saving. Creates a preview in the drafts directory.

command_slug.description: Unique slug for the command (lowercase, 2-64 chars of a-z, 0-9, -, _). Example: 'deploy-app'

description.description: Short description of what the command does

template.description: Command template content. Use $1, $2, etc. for arguments.

function.name: command_apply
function.description: Apply a drafted command, writing it to the commands directory

draft_id.description: The draft ID (command slug) to apply

force_overwrite.description: If true, overwrites existing command with same slug

cleanup_draft.description: If true, removes the draft file after applying

function.name: ast_grep
function.description: Semantic code search using AST patterns - finds code structure, not just text

language.description: Programming language (python, javascript, typescript, rust, go, java, etc.)

path.description: File or directory to search in (defaults to current directory)

replacement.description: Replacement pattern for rewrite mode (e.g. 'async function $NAME($ARGS) { $BODY }')

include_context.description: Include surrounding code context in results

function.name: browse_web
function.description: Browser automation for INTERACTIVE web tasks requiring clicks, form filling, navigation, login, purchases, or complex user interactions. NOT for simple information retrieval, news, or data gathering - use Tavily or Perplexity search tools instead for faster results.

objective.description: Natural language description of what to accomplish on the web

function.name: completion_validation
function.description: Monitor and control the autonomous completion validation system that prevents agents from declaring tasks complete without fulfilling their commitments

session_id.description: Validation session ID for session-specific actions (optional)

function.name: edit_file
function.description: Edit files using exact string replacement - must use Read tool first

file_path.description: Absolute path to the file to edit (must be absolute, not relative)

old_string.description: Exact string to find and replace (must match file contents exactly)

new_string.description: Replacement string (must be different from old_string)

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences of old_string (default: false)

function.name: edit_image_gemini
function.description: Edit existing images using Gemini 2.5 Flash Image. State-of-the-art targeted transformations, background blurring, stain removal, pose alteration, and colorization with natural language instructions.

input_image_path.description: Path to the existing image file to edit. Supports PNG, JPG, JPEG, GIF, and WebP formats.

edit_instructions.description: Natural language instructions for how to modify the image. Be specific about what changes you want (e.g., 'make the sky blue', 'add clouds', 'change the person's shirt to red').

context.description: Additional context about the image or editing goals. This helps maintain consistency across edits.

filename.description: Optional custom filename for the edited image (without extension). If not provided, will auto-generate based on original filename.

function.name: generate_image_gemini
function.description: Generate images using Google's Gemini 2.5 Flash Image model (nano-banana). State-of-the-art image generation with character consistency, multi-image fusion, and enhanced world knowledge.

prompt.description: Creative description of the image to generate. Be as detailed and specific as you want - Gemini understands complex, conversational prompts and can incorporate world knowledge.

additional_details.description: Optional additional context, style preferences, or technical specifications. Will be combined with the main prompt.

filename.description: Optional custom filename (without extension). If not provided, will auto-generate based on prompt

function.name: generate_image_openai
function.description: Generate high-quality images using OpenAI's new GPT-image-1 model (not DALL-E). Supports various styles including coloring book pages, artistic images, and professional graphics. Cost-optimized for medium quality by default.

prompt.description: Detailed description of the image to generate. Be specific about style, composition, colors, and mood.

quality.description: Image quality: 'low', 'medium', 'high', or 'auto'. Medium recommended for cost efficiency

moderation.description: Moderation sensitivity: 'auto' (default, standard filtering) or 'low' (less restrictive filtering)

filename.description: Optional custom filename (without extension). If not provided, will auto-generate based on prompt

function.name: glob
function.description: Fast file pattern matching with glob patterns

pattern.description: Glob pattern to match files against (e.g. '**/*.py', 'src/**/*.js')

path.description: Directory to search in (defaults to current directory)

sort_by_mtime.description: Sort results by modification time (newest first)

head_limit.description: Limit output to first N files

function.name: grep
function.description: Search files with regex patterns and advanced filtering

pattern.description: Regular expression pattern to search for

path.description: File or directory to search in (defaults to current directory)

type.description: File type to search (js, py, rust, go, java, etc.)

context_before.description: Lines to show before each match

context_after.description: Lines to show after each match

head_limit.description: Limit output to first N entries

multiline.description: Enable multiline mode (pattern can span lines)

function.name: list_templates
function.description: List all available project templates with descriptions.

function.name: macos_control
function.description: Comprehensive macOS automation with 513+ pre-built scripts. Prefer: action=list_scripts to browse, then action=kb_script with kb_script_id + params. For simple automation use open_app/send_keys/click/spotlight_search or script (AppleScript/JXA). DO NOT use to open files; use run_command.

action.description: Action to perform: 'kb_script' (use knowledge base script), 'list_scripts' (list all KB scripts), 'open_app', 'send_keys', 'click', 'spotlight_search', 'applescript', 'jxa', 'script' (custom AppleScript/JXA)

target.description: Target for the action (app name, text to type, coordinates, search term, AppleScript/JXA code). Not needed for 'kb_script' or 'list_scripts' actions.

wait_time.description: Time to wait after action in seconds (default: 1.0)

language.description: Script language for 'script' action: 'applescript' (default) or 'jxa' (JavaScript for Automation)

kb_script_id.description: Knowledge base script ID to execute (e.g., 'safari/open_url', 'notes/create_note', 'system/set_volume'). Use 'list_scripts' action to see all available scripts.

params.description: Parameters for knowledge base script execution. Each script may require different params (e.g., {url: 'https://example.com'}, {title: 'My Note', body: 'Note content'}). Check script definition for required params.

function.name: manage_task
function.description: Manage task list with action-based interface (list, create, update, complete)

function.name: multi_edit_file
function.description: Edit files with multiple exact string replacements - must use Read tool first

file_path.description: Absolute path to the file to edit (must be absolute, not relative)

edits.description: List of edit operations, each with old_string, new_string, and optional replace_all

function.name: notebook_edit
function.description: Edit Jupyter notebook cells - insert, delete, or modify cells by ID or index

notebook_path.description: Absolute path to the Jupyter notebook file (.ipynb)

new_source.description: New source content for the cell

cell_id.description: ID of the cell to edit (when inserting, new cell goes after this ID)

function.name: perplexity_search
function.description: AI-powered search with intelligent, curated answers. Best for research, complex questions, analysis, and when you need synthesized information rather than raw search results.

query.description: Research question or complex query requiring AI analysis

search_type.description: Type: 'research' for detailed analysis, 'quick' for fast answers

function.name: project_template
function.description: Create a new project from predefined templates.

template_type.description: Type of template to use (web-app, research, automation).

project_name.description: Name for the new project (kebab-case recommended).

workspace_path.description: Custom workspace path (optional, uses default if not provided).

function.name: quick_update
function.description: Send brief progress updates to keep users informed during long operations

message.description: Brief 1-2 sentence status update (e.g., 'Analyzing files... 50% complete')

duration.description: How long to display the update in seconds (default: 2.0)

function.name: read_file
function.description: Read file contents

function.name: run_command
function.description: Execute shell commands. Use 'open filename' on macOS to open files in default app (e.g., 'open report.md' opens in text editor). Preferred for file operations, git, npm, curl, and all CLI tools.

command.description: Shell command or code to execute

skill_directory.description: Skill directory path for skill-specific .env file loading

function.name: schedule_task
function.description: Schedule recurring or one-time FFT tasks. Agent provides structured timing parameters (schedule_type, hour, minute, etc.) calculated from natural language. Supports one-time execution, daily, weekly, and interval schedules.

workflow.description: Workflow name to replay instead of prompt

profile.description: Profile name to use when running this scheduled task (overrides default).

schedule_rule.description: RFC5545 RRULE string (or multiline DTSTART+RRULE). If provided, schedule_type defaults to 'rrule'.

timezone.description: IANA timezone ID (e.g., 'America/Chicago'). Used with schedule_rule or time_string.

start_datetime.description: ISO 8601 start datetime (used when schedule_rule lacks DTSTART).

weekdays.description: Days to run on: [1,2,3,4,5] for Mon-Fri (0=Sun, 6=Sat)

time_string.description: Natural language time like '21:12 tonight', 'in 5 minutes', '6:35 AM daily', 'weekdays at 9:00'. Parsed automatically into schedule parameters.

function.name: search_code
function.description: Search code using natural language

path.description: Directory or file path to search (defaults to current directory)

function.name: semantic_search
function.description:
Semantic CODE search using natural language and local embeddings (osgrep).
BEST FOR: Codebase concept search in git repositories - 'where is authentication handled?', 'find rate limiting logic', 'how do we validate tools?'.
NOT FOR: Project discovery in ff-terminal-workspace (use glob/grep instead - workspace is not a git repo).
Finds meaning in CODE, not just keywords. Powered by tree-sitter code parsing.

query.description: Natural language query describing what you're looking for (e.g., 'where do we handle rate limiting?')

path.description: Directory to search in (defaults to current directory)

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return

show_content.description: Show full code chunk content instead of snippets

function.name: session_summary
function.description: Create or update a session summary for cross-session continuity. Use at the end of work sessions to leave context for future sessions. Writes to ff-terminal-workspace/session_summary.md with accomplishments, decisions made, and recommended next steps.

action.description: Action to perform: 'write' (create/update summary), 'read' (get current summary), 'append' (add to existing)

accomplishments.description: What was accomplished this session (for 'write' or 'append' actions)

decisions.description: Key decisions made and their rationale (for 'write' or 'append' actions)

next_steps.description: Recommended next steps for the following session (for 'write' or 'append' actions)

context.description: Additional context or notes for the next session (for 'write' or 'append' actions)

project_name.description: Optional project name to associate this summary with

function.name: skill_apply
function.description: Write a drafted skill to disk and refresh the registry.

draft_id.description: The `draft_id` returned by skill_draft (typically the slug).

force_overwrite.description: Set true to replace an existing skill folder.

cleanup_draft.description: Delete the draft after applying the skill.

function.name: skill_documentation
function.description: Generate documentation for existing skills or skill drafts.

skill_slug.description: Skill slug to generate documentation for (leave empty for all skills).

format.description: Output format: markdown, html, or json.

function.name: skill_draft
function.description: Draft a skill manifest and instructions before writing files.

skill_slug.description: Unique slug for the skill (snake_case preferred).

name.description: Human-friendly name for the skill. Defaults to a title-cased slug.

summary.description: Short summary that appears in skill hints.

description.description: Long-form description for the manifest. Defaults to the summary.

instructions.description: Complete instructions that should populate instructions.md.

assets.description: Optional asset files mapped from relative path to file content.

recommended_tools.description: Tool slugs to suggest after the skill loads.

author.description: Optional author metadata for the manifest.

version.description: Optional semantic version for the skill (default 0.1.0).

function.name: skill_import
function.description: Import a skill from URL, Git repository, or local file.

source.description: Skill source: URL, Git repo (https://github.com/user/repo), or local file path.

skill_name.description: Name for imported skill (optional, auto-detected if not provided).

force_overwrite.description: Replace existing skill if it exists.

function.name: skill_loader
function.description: Load a skill's instructions and optional assets when a task needs them.

skill_slug.description: The slug from the skill manifest (e.g., 'rapid_research').

include_assets.description: Set true to include linked templates/assets in the response.

function.name: skill_sequencer
function.description: Execute multi-skill workflows with intelligent sequencing and compatibility checking.

task.description: The complex task requiring multiple skills.

max_skills.description: Maximum number of skills to include in sequence.

force_primary.description: Force a specific skill as primary in the sequence.

function.name: subagent_tool
function.description: Launch specialized AI subagents for parallel execution and task acceleration. Create multiple subagents simultaneously to handle different aspects of complex projects. Each subagent works autonomously with full tool access - perfect for breaking down large tasks into parallel workstreams for maximum efficiency.

description.description: A short, compelling description of the specialist AI (e.g., 'Code Reviewer', 'UI Designer', 'Data Analyst', 'Content Writer')

prompt.description: Detailed task instructions for the AI specialist. Be specific about deliverables and requirements. The subagent has full tool access and will work autonomously.

model_tier.description: Model capability: 'full' (maximum capability, recommended), 'mini' (fast/economical), 'nano' (minimal tasks only)

function.name: tavily_crawl
function.description: Crawl a website from a base URL (depth and page limit configurable)

limit.description: Maximum number of pages to crawl

function.name: tavily_extract
function.description: Extract web page content for specified URLs (supports text and images)

urls.description: List of URLs to extract content from

include_images.description: Whether to include image metadata in extraction

function.name: tavily_map
function.description: Map a site's internal link structure from a base URL

max_depth.description: Maximum depth to discover internal links

function.name: tavily_search
function.description: Fast web search with curated, relevant results. Best for news, facts, current events, and general information retrieval. Much faster than browser automation.

query.description: Search query for current information, news, facts, or data

search_depth.description: Search depth: 'basic' for quick results, 'advanced' for comprehensive

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return (1-20)

function.name: think
function.description: Use this tool as a scratchpad to record reasoning, analyze problems, and maintain context during complex multi-step tasks. Helps prevent losing track of progress and improves decision-making quality.

thought.description: Your reasoning, analysis, or thought to record. Use this to: break down complex problems, analyze tool outputs, verify policy compliance, check progress against goals, or plan next steps.

function.name: TodoWrite
function.description: Manage todo list for multi-step tasks. CRITICAL: Call this to UPDATE task status as you work (pending→in_progress→completed). Don't just create the list at the start - update it throughout execution to show progress. Users see this in real-time.

todos.description: The updated todo list - array of objects with id, content, status (pending/in_progress/completed), priority (high/medium/low)

id.description: Unique identifier for the todo item

function.name: workflow_automation
function.description: Record, save, replay and manage automated workflows. Supports both user-driven workflow creation and AI-powered pattern recognition. Can execute saved workflows with dynamic parameters. Supports macOS control (maintains login sessions) and browser automation modes.

action.description: Action to perform: 'record' (start recording), 'stop' (stop recording), 'replay' (execute workflow), 'list' (show workflows), 'suggest' (get AI suggestions), 'teach' (interactive teaching mode), 'delete' (remove workflow)

workflow_name.description: Name of the workflow to record, replay, or manage

parameters.description: Dynamic parameters for workflow execution (for replay action)

execution_mode.description: Execution mode for replay: 'macos' (uses atomic-level precision macOS control - RECOMMENDED), 'browser' (uses browser-use, fresh sessions), 'legacy' (original simple execution), 'auto' (automatically choose best method)

description.description: Description of the workflow (for record action)

tags.description: Tags for workflow organization (for record action)

function.name: write_file
function.description: Write content to files

content.description: Content to write to the file
